title: Pleurothallis cylindrica
intro: Pleurothallis cylindrica, also known as the cylindrical pleurothallis, is a species of orchid native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues and wounds. Bioactive constituents of Pleurothallis cylindrica include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are infusions and tinctures, often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as skin infections and wounds.
taxonomy: Pleurothallis cylindrica is classified within the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This classification is based on its morphological characteristics and genetic relationships with other orchids. The genus Pleurothallis, to which P. cylindrica belongs, is a group of small, terrestrial orchids. The family Orchidaceae is the largest and most diverse family of flowering plants, with over 30,000 known species. The classification of P. cylindrica is further refined within the subtribe Pleurothallidinae, a group of orchids that are native to the Americas.
